title: Holidays Of Future Passed

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening sequence presumably shows Homer in some mishap
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration and economy, but predictable opening gag

jokes.text: I'll never eat turkey again. Marge, we got any ham?!
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically declares he'll never eat turkey again
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for another meat
jokes.explanation: Good structure with immediate contradiction revealing Homer's gluttony

jokes.text: I'm Santa? Oh, now I'll never die.
jokes.setup: Homer is told he's playing Santa for photos
jokes.punchline: He believes this gives him immortality
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic perfectly fitting Homer's childlike magical thinking

jokes.text: Can't we just send out a picture of the pets dressed like reindeer? We tried that last year. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests using pets as reindeer for photos
jokes.punchline: Reveal they already tried and failed
jokes.explanation: Solid setup-punchline with implied visual of pets in costume disaster

jokes.text: This cycle of jerks has to end.
jokes.punchline: Bart calls his own family lineage a 'cycle of jerks'
jokes.explanation: Dark but insightful family commentary with excellent economy

jokes.text: I wonder what my life would have been like if I'd never seen that movie.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions It's a Wonderful Life is about to start
jokes.punchline: He wonders about alternate reality without seeing it
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the movie's premise while missing the irony entirely

jokes.text: You're two weeks late on the rent, Bart. Not to mention that geography report from 30 years ago.
jokes.setup: Future Bart being confronted about late rent
jokes.punchline: Skinner still remembers a decades-old school assignment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner characterization - petty, bureaucratic, and eternally bitter about student assignments

jokes.text: Don't worry. I got some buyers coming over to look at my good kidney. They grow back, right?
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions selling organs with medical ignorance
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about adult Bart's desperation combined with his continued ignorance

jokes.text: You mean evicted?
jokes.punchline: Bart corrects the terminology for apartments vs. school
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing the confusion of Bart living at school

jokes.text: It smells like dog, but there's no dog.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about going to Dad's place
jokes.punchline: Mysterious dog smell without a dog
jokes.explanation: Vivid detail that paints picture of Bart's sad living situation

jokes.text: Christmas with you is gonna suck! You're wrong. This is gonna be your best Christmas ever. Really? Yeah. Give me some credit. I'll just dump 'em at my mom's.
jokes.setup: Bart promises the best Christmas ever
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he'll dump them elsewhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Bart as a lazy father just like Homer was

jokes.text: You know, we can hear thoughts now. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Kids reveal they can hear thoughts
jokes.punchline: Bart's immediate regret at being caught
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi comedy concept with classic Simpsons family dysfunction

jokes.text: I can't believe we put a man on the Sun, but we can't stop my sneezing!
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions impossible achievement as baseline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist future technology joke with perfect casual delivery

jokes.text: Gee, I'm fine, Mom. Thanks for asking. I'm going online.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks specific question about math test
jokes.punchline: Daughter sarcastically points out lack of general concern
jokes.explanation: Realistic teen snark about overly focused parental questioning

jokes.text: How did my daughter turn into my brother?
jokes.punchline: Recognizes Bart-like traits in her child
jokes.explanation: Good family genetics joke - responsible Lisa horrified at raising a Bart

jokes.text: When we had her, they used only the best genetic material, which meant none of mine. You parked the car. That helped. I guess.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's pathetic contribution to conception
jokes.explanation: Peak Milhouse - self-deprecating and useless, but Lisa's dismissive response adds layers

jokes.text: You could go back to Michigan. It's still under Sharia law. Yeah, but they always make me wear a veil.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to visit Michigan for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of religious law takeover and Milhouse's vanity
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire undercut by Milhouse's shallow concern about appearance

jokes.text: recent research has shown that the umbilical cord is also a vocal cord.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining why pregnant Maggie can't sing
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with medical authority delivering nonsense

jokes.text: Why do you say future? This is now. I meant a week from tomorrow. That's when the new penis gets here.
jokes.punchline: He's literally waiting for penis delivery
jokes.explanation: Unexpected revelation creating awkward misdirection about future enhancement

jokes.text: I just got a message from Maggie in my brain. Ooh, a B-mail!
jokes.setup: Marge mentions brain message from Maggie
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited reaction to 'B-mail' pun
jokes.explanation: Clever future tech wordplay with Homer's childlike enthusiasm

jokes.text: Hey, I got a B-mail, too. 'You have won a valuable prize. Open now.' Don't open it, Homie. It's a virus. Too late. I...
jokes.punchline: Opens obvious virus despite warning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - impulsive and gullible, updating spam for brain technology

jokes.text: Ho... ho... ho.
jokes.punchline: Now stuck saying Santa phrases robotically
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective payoff to virus setup with Santa callback

jokes.text: Yeah, after I stopped drinking, this was a way to stay close to my bottles. I'd smash 'em all for one lousy beer!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his bottle ship collection
jokes.punchline: Would destroy hobby for one drink
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest look at Homer's relationship with alcohol and hobbies

jokes.text: Oh, man, I can't believe they still haven't figured out a way to detangle Christmas lights.
jokes.punchline: Christmas lights still can't be untangled
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about unsolvable holiday problems persisting into future

jokes.text: Mom's boyfriend is good at detangling them. Well, maybe Mom should marry him. She did. We weren't supposed to tell you.
jokes.punchline: Reveal secret remarriage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from practical help to devastating family news

jokes.text: Hey, Grampa, you gonna build a snowman? No, I just like to dress up to eat my carrots and smoke.
jokes.setup: Assumption Grampa is playing in snow
jokes.punchline: He's just using costume for mundane activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa - practical and eccentric with great economy

jokes.text: That's why after Homer accidentally killed Edna, I married Maude's ghost. There is no God, Neddy. It's just an empty meaningless void. Isn't she pretty?
jokes.punchline: Ghost delivers nihilistic philosophy while Ned stays cheerful
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy juxtaposing Ned's optimism with ghost's existential despair, plus casual mention of Homer killing Edna

jokes.text: There is no God, Neddy. It's just an empty meaningless void. / Isn't she pretty?
jokes.punchline: Ned finds her nihilistic despair beautiful
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor contrasting Ned's faith with ghost Maude's atheism

jokes.text: Oh, ma'am, you really shouldn't teleport when you're pregnant. I'm afraid your only choice is... air travel.
jokes.punchline: Air travel presented as worse alternative
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection presenting current air travel as future punishment

jokes.text: If you are seated in an exit row, please hold the door shut for the duration of the flight.
jokes.punchline: Instructions to literally hold door closed
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on airline safety suggesting planes barely hold together

jokes.text: 69% of the original Cher is playing. Sure, I'd love to... only not with you and not that.
jokes.setup: Milhouse suggests concert with cyborg Cher
jokes.punchline: Lisa rejects both him and the event
jokes.explanation: Future celebrity reconstruction concept with Lisa's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Sometimes I wish strangling your kid was still legal. Not since they passed Homer's Law.
jokes.punchline: Homer's behavior created actual legislation
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback transforming Homer's signature move into social commentary

jokes.text: Suicide pills? Mohawk gel? 20-year-old Business Week? Turkey and Brie wrap?
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre and depressing options
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal to absurd airline offerings

jokes.text: My daughter thinks I'm a ruthless tyrant, like Hitler or Prince Harry. Bloody Harry. He brought back beheading in a big way.
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing herself to historical tyrants
jokes.punchline: Future Prince Harry as actual dictator
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire imagining royal family member as future dictator

jokes.text: Bloody Harry. He brought back beheading in a big way.
jokes.setup: Reference to Prince Harry as tyrant
jokes.punchline: He literally brought back medieval executions
jokes.explanation: Continues the Prince Harry joke with dark escalation

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. I have my invisibility cloak. Now, where did I put it? Oh.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions having invisibility cloak for safety
jokes.punchline: Can't find it because it's invisible
jokes.explanation: Classic setup-punchline with logical problem of invisible objects

jokes.text: What are you after? We're a cashless society.
jokes.punchline: Criminal confused by lack of physical money
jokes.explanation: Good future crime joke showing outdated criminal methods

jokes.text: Hey, didn't I go to jail with your brother?
jokes.punchline: Driver recognizes passenger through family criminal connections
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback to ongoing Simpson family dysfunction

jokes.text: Well, looks like I'm gonna have to do this the old-fashioned way. Gloves, hospital.
jokes.setup: Maggie in labor without proper hospital
jokes.punchline: Voice-commanding medical supplies to appear
jokes.explanation: Future technology joke about voice-activated medical delivery

jokes.text: Should I have married Nelson? 'Cause we still talk on the phone. Nelson calls you? Well... someone calls someone.
jokes.setup: Lisa wondering about alternate relationship choices
jokes.punchline: Awkward revelation about ongoing contact with ex
jokes.explanation: Realistic marital tension about maintaining contact with exes

jokes.text: Did you bake any? Yes, I did. And they were some of the best cookies ever made in this house. But that is not the point.
jokes.setup: Lisa angry about being told to bake cookies
jokes.punchline: Admits she actually did and they were excellent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - follows through even when protesting the principle

jokes.text: Maybe it's the court-mandated sincerity chip I got in my brain, but, Lis, you're the person I always wanted to be.
jokes.setup: Bart about to give sincere compliment
jokes.punchline: Attributes sincerity to court-ordered brain implant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart characterization - genuine emotion undermined by criminal backstory

jokes.text: Our consciousness was a secret for thousands of years. Then one pine tree had to open his sappy mouth.
jokes.setup: Trees are revealed to be conscious
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'sappy' referring both to tree sap and being foolish
jokes.explanation: Clever pun with absurdist premise about tree consciousness

jokes.text: Our consciousness was a secret for thousands of years. Then one pine tree had to open his sappy mouth.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'sappy' while breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with environmental consciousness theme

jokes.text: Who's Krusty? Only the funniest man in the world. I've never let go of my fax machine. Don't ask me why. I like the sound of a fax coming through. Especially if it's a nice deli menu.
jokes.setup: Kids don't know who Krusty is
jokes.punchline: Krusty's pathetic attachment to obsolete technology
jokes.explanation: Good Krusty characterization - pathetic and nostalgic for mundane past

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but there's no room at the in... patient facility. A star in the east... Let me see. We do have a little room in the manger. I mean, mangier wing.
jokes.punchline: Christmas nativity reference with bad pun recovery
jokes.explanation: Christmas reference joke with awkward pun correction

jokes.text: How could he be such a cool grandfather when he's such a lousy father? People learn from their mistakes. And your father made so many mistakes.
jokes.setup: Lisa wondering about Homer's improvement as grandfather
jokes.punchline: Marge's backhanded compliment about Homer's extensive errors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - supportive but brutally honest about Homer's history

jokes.text: Make me a Bloody Mary, dollface. No, Selma. Even a robot built only to love you cannot love you. I am leaving with your sister's concu-droid.
jokes.setup: Selma ordering drink from robot boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Robot rejects her for Patty's robot
jokes.explanation: Brutal joke about Selma being unlovable even to programmed robots

jokes.text: Oh, Sanjay, how I wish that runaway jerky wagon killed me instead of you. Oh, I miss you, too, brother.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous cause of death - jerky wagon
jokes.explanation: Absurdist death by snack food wagon with touching brother dynamic

jokes.text: Sometimes a mother's job is to butt in. Lisa? Butt out! This is it-- I'm going into the Ultranet to save my daughter.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's rejection leads to dramatic digital intervention
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with mother-daughter conflict escalation

jokes.text: Martin Prince is now Marcia Princess?
jokes.setup: Lisa seeing friend requests in the Ultranet
jokes.punchline: Martin has transitioned and changed his name
jokes.explanation: Future callback with social commentary, handled respectfully

jokes.text: Is your marriage still dead and unfulfilled? Not a good time.
jokes.punchline: Blunt question about Lisa's marriage at worst moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - blunt and inappropriate timing

jokes.text: Google, even though you've enslaved half the world, you're still a damn fine search engine.
jokes.setup: Marge needs to search for something
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about corporate dystopia
jokes.explanation: Sharp tech dystopia satire with practical acceptance

jokes.text: Hi, Bart! Hey, Ralph. I heard you died. I got cloneded.
jokes.punchline: Ralph's death and resurrection treated casually with mispronunciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph - treats death casually with signature mispronunciation

jokes.text: Hey, Lenny. I'm Carl. Don't you remember? You came to the brain-switching ceremony.
jokes.punchline: Reveals complex brain-switching backstory
jokes.explanation: Absurdist sci-fi concept explaining identity confusion

jokes.text: Because I wanted to get back together with my wife who was sleeping with Carl at the time. Turns out she had switched brains with a monkey on a Japanese game show.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd relationship complications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurdist future relationship problems

jokes.text: Quit making me fat!
jokes.punchline: Blames customer for his own serving choices
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe - shirking responsibility with bartender logic

jokes.text: Could somebody FedEx me a prayer mat? And quick!
jokes.punchline: Casual delivery request for religious item
jokes.explanation: Good future delivery joke with religious panic

jokes.text: What, me flashing my boobs on unripemelons.com?
jokes.setup: Marge worried about what she'd find online
jokes.punchline: Daughter's casual mention of inappropriate website
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable teen rebellion with creative website name

jokes.text: He froze himself because he was sick and there was no cure. Are they working on one? Oh, they found it-- but don't tell him. This is way cheaper than a nursing home.
jokes.setup: Explanation of why Grampa is frozen
jokes.punchline: Family keeping cure secret for financial reasons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy about elder care economics with perfect Simpson family dysfunction

jokes.text: Eh, I don't see what's great about 'em.
jokes.punchline: Grampa takes 'great' literally and dismisses them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa - literal-minded and unimpressed with family

jokes.text: Boys, I'm a deadbeat dad, I live in a school, it's Christmas. The only thing worth anything in my life is you.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to reconnect with his sons
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of his failures leading to genuine emotion
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment showing adult Bart's self-awareness and growth

jokes.text: You've taught us the meaning of Christmas, which schools are forbidden to tell us anymore.
jokes.setup: Son responding to father's emotional speech
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to educational restrictions
jokes.explanation: Political commentary on separation of church and school

jokes.text: You quit drinking like a coward! The one thing you were good at...
jokes.punchline: Drinking described as Homer's one talent
jokes.explanation: Dark family dysfunction joke with Grampa's twisted parental pride

jokes.text: So, who's the father? Eh, it doesn't matter. If there's one day a year to give unwed mothers a break, it's Christmas.
jokes.punchline: Homer's Christmas spirit applied to family scandal
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's evolved perspective on family

jokes.text: Hmm, funny how they evolved and we didn't.
jokes.punchline: Homer observes that pets evolved while humans didn't
jokes.explanation: Nice closing observation about role reversal between pets and humans

jokes.text: Hmm, funny how they evolved and we didn't.
jokes.punchline: Homer noting pets' superior development
jokes.explanation: Good closing observation about role reversal in future evolution

tmdb_overview: Thirty years in the future, Bart, Lisa and their children spend the holidays at Marge and Homer's house.
